title: Listen Up
tagline: Big books, distilled. Every key idea and every story in one deep listen, so you get the whole book without reading it. New titles every week.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.blurb: Tiny one-percent changes compound into remarkable results. The Four Laws for building good habits and breaking bad ones, with the British-cycling and two-minute-rule stories.

books.title: The Psychology of Money
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.blurb: Doing well with money is about behaviour, not brains. Luck versus risk, knowing what's 'enough', and why compounding rewards the patient, through Gates, Buffett and the man-in-the-car paradox.
chapters: Wealth is what you don't see

books.title: Shoe Dog
books.author: Phil Knight
books.blurb: Phil Knight's raw, thrilling memoir of building Nike from a car-boot shoe hustle into a global icon, through constant near-bankruptcy and a refusal to quit.

books.title: Never Split the Difference
books.author: Chris Voss
books.blurb: An FBI hostage negotiator's playbook for everyday deals: tactical empathy, mirroring, calibrated questions, why 'no' beats 'yes' and how to get to 'that's right.'

books.title: Bad Blood
books.author: John Carreyrou
books.blurb: The jaw-dropping true story of Theranos and Elizabeth Holmes, a nine-billion-dollar Silicon Valley fraud that reads like a thriller. Hype versus substance, laid bare.

books.title: How I Built This
books.author: Guy Raz
books.blurb: The hard-won lessons of the world's great founders, distilled from hundreds of interviews: luck, grit, the leap of faith, and surviving the valley of death.

books.title: The Mom Test
books.author: Rob Fitzpatrick
books.blurb: How to talk to customers when everyone's too polite to tell you the truth, and learn whether your idea is any good before you build it.

books.title: $100M Offers
books.author: Alex Hormozi
books.blurb: Craft an offer so good people feel stupid saying no. The value equation, pricing, and stacking value until the price becomes an afterthought.

books.title: The Power of Habit
books.author: Charles Duhigg
books.blurb: Why habits exist and how they change. The cue-routine-reward loop, keystone habits, and the true stories, Pepsodent, Alcoa, Target, behind the science.

books.title: Essentialism
books.author: Greg McKeown
books.blurb: Less, but better. The disciplined pursuit of only what truly matters, and the graceful art of saying no to everything else.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.blurb: Focused, distraction-free work is a superpower that's getting rare. How to train deep concentration and produce more in far less time.

books.title: The ONE Thing
books.author: Gary Keller
books.blurb: Extraordinary results come from narrowing your focus. Find the one thing that makes everything else easier or unnecessary.

books.title: Zero to One
books.author: Peter Thiel
books.blurb: Peter Thiel on building the future. Why real progress means going from zero to one, creating something new, not copying what already works.

books.title: The Lean Startup
books.author: Eric Ries
books.blurb: Build, measure, learn. How to test ideas fast, avoid building things nobody wants, and steer with validated learning instead of guesswork.

books.title: Creativity, Inc.
books.author: Ed Catmull
books.blurb: Ed Catmull on running Pixar, building a culture where creativity survives, candor thrives, and good ideas beat the fear of failure.

books.title: Influence
books.author: Robert Cialdini
books.blurb: The six psychological triggers that make us say yes, reciprocity, scarcity, authority, consistency, liking, and social proof. The classic on persuasion.

books.title: Made to Stick
books.author: Chip & Dan Heath
books.blurb: Why some ideas stick and others vanish. The six traits, simple, unexpected, concrete, credible, emotional, story, that make a message unforgettable.

books.title: Contagious
books.author: Jonah Berger
books.blurb: Why things catch on. The six principles behind word of mouth, and why some products and ideas spread like wildfire.

books.title: Same as Ever
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.blurb: What never changes. Timeless truths about risk, greed, and human behaviour that stay the same no matter how the world shifts.

books.title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
books.author: Robert Kiyosaki
books.blurb: The mindset shift that changed how millions think about money. Assets versus liabilities, and why financial education beats a paycheck.

books.title: The Richest Man in Babylon
books.author: George S. Clason
books.blurb: Timeless money wisdom told through ancient Babylonian parables. Pay yourself first, live below your means, and let gold work while you sleep.

books.title: One Up On Wall Street
books.author: Peter Lynch
books.blurb: Legendary investor Peter Lynch on why ordinary people can beat the pros, by investing in what they already know and understand.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.blurb: Two systems drive your mind, one fast and intuitive, one slow and deliberate, and the hidden biases that quietly steer every decision you make.

books.title: Outliers
books.author: Malcolm Gladwell
books.blurb: What really makes high achievers? The hidden advantages, timing, and the ten-thousand-hour idea behind extraordinary success.

books.title: Range
books.author: David Epstein
books.blurb: Why generalists triumph in a specialized world. How breadth, cross-training, and late starts often beat narrow early expertise.

books.title: Think Again
books.author: Adam Grant
books.blurb: The power of knowing what you don't know. Rethinking, updating your beliefs, and the surprising joy of being wrong.

books.title: Thinking in Bets
books.author: Annie Duke
books.blurb: A world-champion poker player on deciding under uncertainty, separating the quality of a choice from how it happened to turn out.

books.title: Why We Sleep
books.author: Matthew Walker
books.blurb: Sleep is the single most powerful thing you can do to reset brain and body. What it does for you, and the real damage of going without it.

books.title: Outlive
books.author: Peter Attia
books.blurb: The science and art of longevity. How to add healthy years, not just years, by training now for the 'marginal decade' at the end of life.

books.title: Breath
books.author: James Nestor
books.blurb: The lost art of breathing. How the simple way you breathe reshapes your health, sleep, and calm, backed by science and daring self-experiments.

books.title: $100M Leads
books.author: Alex Hormozi
books.blurb: The sequel to $100M Offers: how to get a flood of people who want to buy. Lead magnets, the core-four outreach methods, and turning strangers into customers at scale.

books.title: $100M Money Models
books.author: Alex Hormozi
books.blurb: Structure your offers and pricing so customers fund your own growth, the money models that let a business acquire customers profitably and scale fast.

books.title: How to Make Money in Stocks
books.author: William O'Neil
books.blurb: The classic CAN SLIM system, how the biggest stock-market winners behaved before they soared, and the disciplined rules for buying, selling, and cutting losses.

books.title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
books.author: Ben Horowitz
books.blurb: There are no recipes for the hard parts. Ben Horowitz on nearly losing his company three times, firing friends, wartime leadership, and why there are no silver bullets, only lead bullets.

books.title: Mindset
books.author: Carol S. Dweck
books.blurb: Do you believe ability is something you have, or something you build? The research behind fixed and growth mindsets, and the tiny word 'yet' that changes everything.

books.title: The Millionaire Next Door
books.author: Thomas J. Stanley and William D. Danko
books.blurb: The people who look rich usually aren't, and the ones who are drive used cars. The landmark study of how ordinary Americans quietly got wealthy, and the formula to score yourself.

books.title: Building a StoryBrand
books.author: Donald Miller
books.blurb: Your customer is the hero, not you. The seven-part story framework that makes people finally understand what you sell, because if you confuse, you lose.

books.title: Can't Hurt Me
books.author: David Goggins
books.blurb: From a 300-pound exterminator to a Navy SEAL running 100 miles on broken feet. The accountability mirror, the cookie jar, and the 40% rule that says you're barely getting started.
